{{Short description|4th or 5th century Japanese burial mound}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|38.238197|140.914447|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji ware]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)]] *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Tomizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.miyagi.jp/site/sitei/kuni-siseki09.html Miyagi Prefecture Official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.sendai.jp/waka-katsudo/wakabayashiku/machizukuri/miryoku/kofun.html Sendai City official site] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|4th or 5th century {{Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|38.238197|140.914447|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji ware]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji ware]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.

[[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.

It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji ware]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

{{Short description|4th or 5th century {{Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|38.238197|140.914447|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji ware]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tōmizuka Kofun]][[en:Tōmizuka Kofun]]

{{Short description|4th or 5th century {{Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|38.238197|140.914447|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji ware]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)]] *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]

[[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji ware]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.

The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.

{{Short description|4th or 5th century ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|38.238197|140.914447|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)]] *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tomizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.miyagi.jp/site/sitei/kuni-siseki09.html Miyagi Prefecture Official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.sendai.jp/waka-katsudo/wakabayashiku/machizukuri/miryoku/kofun.html Sendai City official site] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|4th or 5th century ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|38.238197|140.914447|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)]] *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tomizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.miyagi.jp/site/sitei/kuni-siseki09.html Miyagi Prefecture Official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.sendai.jp/waka-katsudo/wakabayashiku/machizukuri/miryoku/kofun.html Sendai City official site] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tōmizuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tōmizuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Tōmizuka Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Tōmizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

{{Short description|4th or 5th century ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|38.238197|140.914447|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

{{Short description|4th or 5th century ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|38.238197|140.914447|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)]] *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tomizuka Kofun]][[en:Tōmizuka Kofun]][[ja:遠見塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11642380]] Wikidata name: Tōmizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|4th or 5th century ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.238211|N|140.914444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakabayashi-ku|qid=Q1131743}} | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sendai, Miyagi|Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[:en:Kasuminome Air Field|Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[:en:Japan National Route 4|Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)]] *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]

==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[:en:Kasuminome Air Field|Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[:en:Japan National Route 4|Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tomizuka Kofun]][[en:Tōmizuka Kofun]][[ja:遠見塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11642380]] Wikidata name: Tōmizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|4th or 5th century ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.238211|N|140.914444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakabayashi-ku|WD=Q1131743}} | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sendai, Miyagi|Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[:en:Kasuminome Air Field|Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[:en:Japan National Route 4|Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)]] *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tomizuka Kofun]][[en:Tōmizuka Kofun]][[ja:遠見塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11642380]] Wikidata name: Tōmizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|4th or 5th century ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.238211|N|140.914444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakabayashi-ku|WD=Q1131743}} | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sendai, Miyagi|Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[:en:Kasuminome Air Field|Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[:en:Japan National Route 4|Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

The site is a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[:en:Kasuminome Air Field|Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[:en:Japan National Route 4|Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[:en:Kasuminome Air Field|Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[:en:Japan National Route 4|Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].

period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the [[:en:Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|Wakabayashi ward]] of the city of [[:en:Sendai, Miyagi|Sendai, Miyagi]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby [[:en:Kasuminome Air Field|Kasuminome Air Field]]. [[:en:Japan National Route 4|Japan National Route 4]] also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Sendai Station (Miyagi)|Sendai Station]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)]] *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]

The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of {{ill|Sendai Station (Miyagi)|en|Sendai Station (Miyagi)|WD=Q801429|lt=Sendai Station}}.

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the {{ill|Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|en|Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1131743|lt=Wakabayashi ward}} of the city of [[:en:Sendai, Miyagi|Sendai, Miyagi]] in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby {{ill|Kasuminome Air Field|en|Kasuminome Air Field|WD=Q6374904|lt=Kasuminome Air Field}}. {{ill|Japan National Route 4|en|Japan National Route 4|WD=Q1055023|lt=Japan National Route 4}} also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of {{ill|Sendai Station (Miyagi)|en|Sendai Station (Miyagi)|WD=Q801429|lt=Sendai Station}}.

The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby {{ill|Kasuminome Air Field|en|Kasuminome Air Field|WD=Q6374904|lt=Kasuminome Air Field|1=Kasuminome Air Field}}. {{ill|Japan National Route 4|en|Japan National Route 4|WD=Q1055023|lt=Japan National Route 4|1=Japan National Route 4}} also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of {{ill|Sendai Station (Miyagi)|en|Sendai Station (Miyagi)|WD=Q801429|lt=Sendai Station|1=Sendai Station (Miyagi)}}.

{{Short description|4th or 5th century ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomizuka Kofun | native_name = 遠見塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Toomizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.238211|N|140.914444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakabayashi-ku|WD=Q1131743|1=Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 110 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1968, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park} | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Tōmizuka Kofun'''|遠見塚古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the {{ill|Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|en|Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1131743|lt=Wakabayashi ward|1=Wakabayashi-ku, Sendai}} of the city of [[:en:Sendai, Miyagi|Sendai, Miyagi]] in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was constructed in the early to middle [[Kofun period]], or around the late 4th to early 5th century AD, as the tomb of one or more of the ''[[gōzoku]]'' ruling the Miyagi Plain region. The site is a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204703|title=遠見塚古墳 とおみづかこふん|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tōmizuka ''Kofun'' is located on a natural embankment on the northern bank of the Hirose River at an altitude of about 10 meters, it is the fifth largest tomb in the Tōhoku region and the second largest in Miyagi prefecture after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]]. The tumulus has a total length of about 110 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 63 meters and height of 6.5 meters. The square portion has a width of 37 meters and height of 2.5 meters. While this keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a characteristic shape, it is unusual is that the square front part is extremely lower than the round rear part. The tumulus is surrounded by an irregularly shaped moat with a width ranging from 10 meters to more than 40 meters in places.
The surrounding area the southern Sendai Plain is noted for extensive {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} remains. The ''kofun'' was excavated by the Sendai City Board of Education in 1968 and 1972, with further work conducted in 1975 in preparation for the creation of a park. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. The circular mound was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two clay sarcophagus which are presumed to have each held a bamboo-shaped wooden coffin. The number of grave goods found was extremely few for the size of the ''kofun'', and were all found in the east sarcophagus. Only one quartz cylindrical bead, four glass balls and 18 pieces of a black-lacquered bamboo comb were excavated. Fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery used for ritual purposes were also found. These artifacts are preserved at the Sendai City Museum.
In 1947, when Japan was [[:en:Occupied Japan|under American occupation]], half of the circular portion of the tumulus was destroyed for dirt to expand nearby {{ill|Kasuminome Air Field|en|Kasuminome Air Field|WD=Q6374904|lt=Kasuminome Air Field|1=Kasuminome Air Field}}. {{ill|Japan National Route 4|en|Japan National Route 4|WD=Q1055023|lt=Japan National Route 4|1=Japan National Route 4}} also cuts across the site diagonally, truncating one edge of the square portion of the tumulus. The site is now preserved as a public park.
The site is located approximately 3.5 kilometers southeast of {{ill|Sendai Station (Miyagi)|en|Sendai Station (Miyagi)|WD=Q801429|lt=Sendai Station|1=Sendai Station (Miyagi)}}.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|WD=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}} *[[Raijinyama Kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline|Tomizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.miyagi.jp/site/sitei/kuni-siseki09.html Miyagi Prefecture Official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.sendai.jp/waka-katsudo/wakabayashiku/machizukuri/miryoku/kofun.html Sendai City official site] {{in lang|ja}}
